what_it_does: This file exports a channel-scoped recall API: a `recall` function plus `RecallParams` and `RecallHit` types. It wraps `searchConversations` by adding structural filters (`session_ids`, `sources`, `project`), resolving the embedding axis, and returning a stable, fabric-facing result shape with canonical provenance, full exchange text, and explicit nullable fields.
observations: The `recall` function is a thin wrapper over `searchConversations` from `search.ts`.
It adds model-independent structural scopes: `session_ids` (channel), `sources` (capture-source), and `project` (exact match).
An empty array for `session_ids` or `sources` is treated as an explicitly empty set returning `[]`, not a silent fall-through.
The embedding axis is resolved once via `getLens`/`lensVecSpec` so every hit can be stamped with the exact axis/space used.
Returned hits include full user+assistant `text`, not just the snippet.
Provenance is scheme-aware: if `sessionId` already contains `://` (e.g. `board://<id>`, `clone://...`), it is emitted verbatim; otherwise `provenanceAddress` builds `exchange://<sid>/<i>`.
Nullable fields (`score`, `session_id`, `summary`) are coalesced to `null` to keep JSON contract stability.
Comments use architecture metaphors: '5th wire', 'board wire', 'C1 same-project rung'.
standouts: Generalized scheme-aware provenance rule that treats any `sessionId` containing `://` as a canonical flat address.
Explicit `null` emission for nullable fields to avoid downstream undefined-key issues.
Structural filters are kept independent of the embedding axis/model.
conventions: Heavy JSDoc comments explaining architectural intent and wire names.
Explicit empty-array semantics (no silent unscoped fallback).
Per-axis golden rule: query embeds and KNN run in the same resolved axis/space.
Canonical provenance as the join key for downstream addressing.
concerns: Behavior depends entirely on `searchConversations`, `getLens`, `lensVecSpec`, and `provenanceAddress`, which are not defined in this file.
`sessionId` is overloaded: it may be a bare UUID or a flat scheme address like `board://<id>`.
No local validation of `session_ids`, `sources`, or `project` values.
Full `text` is built with a fixed `\n\n` separator and could include stray whitespace if messages are empty.
The comment claims cosine similarity is in `[-1, 1]`, but typical cosine similarity for normalized embeddings is `[0, 1]`; the file does not verify normalization.
No local error handling for unknown axis or failed embedding resolution.
notes: A stable, architecture-labeled recall entrypoint that turns semantic search results into canonical, channel-scoped, full-text hits.
questions: How exactly does `searchConversations` compose the `session_ids`, `sources`, and `project` filters together?
What does `lensVecSpec` return, and how is the axis registry maintained?
Why is `sessionId` used to carry flat scheme addresses like `board://<id>` instead of a dedicated provenance field?
What is the 'C1 delegation' contract referenced in the comments?
How does `mode: 'both'` merge vector and text results when text rows lack a score?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a clean wrapper that adds channel/source/project structural scoping and a stable result shape over the underlying semantic search.
apparent_themes: Structural scoping independent of embedding model/axis.
Canonical, dereferenceable provenance as a join key.
Explicit emptiness and explicit nullability for contract stability.
Per-axis golden rule for embedding and search.
Passthrough of sidechain opt-in without local semantics.
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide a single, predictable recall surface for downstream 'Company' consumers, where channels, sources, and projects are filtered structurally while the semantic ranking stays axis-local, and every hit carries a canonical address and full content for further reasoning.
novelty: The generalized provenance rule that detects any flat scheme address in `sessionId` and emits it verbatim, unifying board, clone, and future non-conversation sources through one path rather than source-specific branches.
contribution: A recall entrypoint that adds channel/source/project structural filters, resolves and stamps the embedding axis, canonicalizes provenance across conversation and non-conversation sources, returns full exchange text, and emits explicit nulls to keep downstream JSON contracts stable.
summary_for_embedding: `recall.ts` defines a channel-scoped recall wrapper that enriches semantic search results with structural filters, axis metadata, full exchange text, and canonical scheme-aware provenance.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to provide a stable, fabric-facing recall API for scoped memory retrieval across sessions, sources, and projects while remaining neutral to the embedding axis.

symbols.does: Describes the input contract for a scoped recall query.
symbols.description: `RecallParams` is an interface that collects the semantic query, result limit, embedding axis, retrieval mode, and structural scopes (`session_ids`, `sources`, `project`, `is_sidechain`). It encodes the rule that omitting a scope means unscoped recall, while an empty array means an explicitly empty result set.

symbols.does: Describes one ranked recall result returned to callers.
symbols.description: `RecallHit` is an interface for a stable, fabric-facing result row. It carries cosine `score`, canonical `provenance`, full exchange `text`, the searched `axis`, capture `source`, `project`, `timestamp`, archive location, line range, preview `snippet`, and optional `summary`, with nullable fields typed as `number | null` or `string | null`.

symbols.code_id: code://company/recollection/src/recall.ts::recall | function | export async function recall(params: RecallParams): Promise<RecallHit[]> {
symbols.does: Executes a scoped semantic recall and normalizes results into `RecallHit` objects.
symbols.description: The `recall` function resolves the embedding axis, builds a `SearchOptions` object from the structural scopes, calls `searchConversations`, and maps each raw `SearchResult` into a `RecallHit`. It constructs full exchange text, canonicalizes provenance by detecting scheme addresses in `sessionId`, and coalesces nullable fields to `null` for JSON contract stability.
